summary: One More Legend is a deterministic text roguelite whose headless engine powers a browser game, simulation harnesses, and a stateless ChatGPT MCP app.

reviews.summary: Initial map established the deterministic engine boundary, thin clients, balance harnesses, MCP replay model, and dual-surface deployment.

throughCommit.subject: Differentiate roads and improve decision feedback

questions.question: When should combat emit a first-class FightResult instead of asking telemetry to reconstruct one from prose logs?
questions.whyItMatters: The current parser is observational and intentionally non-invasive, but browser and MCP battle reports need a stable engine contract.

questions.question: How should run tokens identify the exact engine ruleset once multiple deployed versions can coexist?
questions.whyItMatters: A token stores seed and choices rather than state, so replay correctness depends on reconstructing under compatible rules.

questions.question: Which generated game artifacts should remain committed, and which should be deployment-only outputs?
questions.whyItMatters: Keeping the game playable as a single checked-in file is useful, but duplicated generated surfaces increase synchronization risk.

areas.name: Deterministic game engine
areas.purpose: Owns run state, choices, combat, gains, progression, and seeded randomness without depending on a browser, timer, or external service.
areas.verdict: Excellent architectural center; the explicit replay invariant makes every client and simulation easier to trust

walkthrough.title: Follow one choice through a reproducible run
walkthrough.outcome: See how the same seed and choice history drive the browser, bots, and ChatGPT without copying game rules into any client.

steps.label: Create the complete initial state
steps.explanation: OML.newRun builds a run from a seed, road, and explicit options while storing the RNG state inside GameState.
steps.invariant: No engine outcome depends on DOM state, timers, or Math.random.

steps.label: Expose only legal decisions
steps.explanation: OML.getOptions derives the numbered choices for the current phase, including the stable ordering consumed by bots.
steps.invariant: Every client receives the same options for the same reconstructed state.

steps.label: Advance and remember the choice
steps.explanation: OML.step resolves one option through the shared rules and appends its zero-based index to the run history.
steps.invariant: Replaying the recorded choices under the same rules reproduces the same log and score.

steps.label: Prove replay outside the UI
steps.explanation: The determinism check creates a second run from the original seed and choices, then compares its log and score.
steps.invariant: A rules change that breaks reproducibility fails before balance results are trusted.

areas.name: Browser decision client
areas.purpose: Renders the hero sheet, odds, decisions, chronicle, persistent Hall, and mobile-first feedback while delegating game rules to the engine.
areas.verdict: Thin in rules and rich in feedback—the right trade for this game

areas.name: Simulation and balance harnesses
areas.purpose: Replays thousands of deterministic runs through named bot strategies to measure win bands, dead turns, techniques, and Long Road outcomes before shipping rule changes.
areas.verdict: Unusually strong for a small game; balance intent is executable instead of anecdotal

areas.name: ChatGPT MCP game surface
areas.purpose: Exposes start, inspect, and choose tools plus a live widget while reconstructing runs from bounded tokens instead of maintaining server sessions.
areas.verdict: Elegant stateless adapter; engine-version compatibility is the main boundary to watch

areas.name: Dual-surface build and deployment
areas.purpose: Compiles one engine into a self-contained browser game and a bundled MCP service, then deploys both from one Vercel project.
areas.verdict: Pragmatic and legible, with deliberate generated-artifact duplication

areas.name: Fight pressure telemetry
areas.purpose: Derives rounds, health pressure, damage, morale, techniques, and observational grades from combat logs without changing gameplay behavior.
areas.verdict: Useful first slice; intentionally transitional until FightResult becomes an engine contract
